25: // AuditBroker is used to provide a single ingest interface to auditable

42: // Register is used to add new audit backend to the broker

47: 		a.Lock()

71: 	_, ok := a.backends[name]
72: 	return ok
73: }

99: // log the given request and that *at least one* succeeds.
100: func (a *AuditBroker) LogRequest(ctx context.Context, in *logical.LogInput, headersConfig *AuditedHeadersConfig) (ret error) {
101: 	defer metrics.MeasureSince([]string{"audit", "log_request"}, time.Now())
102: 	a.RLock()

120: 		}
121: 
122: 		ret = retErr.ErrorOrNil()
123: 		failure := float32(0.0)
124: 		if ret != nil {
125: 			failure = 1.0
126: 		}
127: 		metrics.IncrCounter([]string{"audit", "log_request_failure"}, failure)
128: 	}()
129: 
130: 	// All logged requests must have an identifier
131: 	//if req.ID == "" {

133: 	//	retErr = multierror.Append(retErr, fmt.Errorf("missing identifier in request object: %s", req.Path))

137: 	headers := in.Request.Headers
138: 	defer func() {
139: 		in.Request.Headers = headers
140: 	}()
141: 
142: 	// Ensure at least one backend logs
143: 	anyLogged := false
144: 	for name, be := range a.backends {
145: 		in.Request.Headers = nil
146: 		transHeaders, thErr := headersConfig.ApplyConfig(ctx, headers, be.backend.GetHash)
147: 		if thErr != nil {
148: 			a.logger.Error("backend failed to include headers", "backend", name, "error", thErr)
149: 			continue
150: 		}
151: 		in.Request.Headers = transHeaders
152: 
153: 		start := time.Now()
154: 		lrErr := be.backend.LogRequest(ctx, in)
155: 		metrics.MeasureSince([]string{"audit", name, "log_request"}, start)
156: 		if lrErr != nil {
157: 			a.logger.Error("backend failed to log request", "backend", name, "error", lrErr)
158: 		} else {

160: 		}
161: 	}

163: 		retErr = multierror.Append(retErr, fmt.Errorf("no audit backend succeeded in logging the request"))

45: func (c *Core) generateAuditTestProbe() (*logical.LogInput, error) {
46: 	requestId, err := uuid.GenerateUUID()
47: 	if err != nil {
48: 		return nil, err
49: 	}
50: 	return &logical.LogInput{
51: 		Type: "request",
52: 		Auth: nil,
53: 		Request: &logical.Request{
54: 			ID:        requestId,
55: 			Operation: "update",
56: 			Path:      "sys/audit/test",
57: 		},
58: 		Response: nil,
59: 		OuterErr: nil,
60: 	}, nil
61: }

63: // enableAudit is used to enable a new audit backend
64: func (c *Core) enableAudit(ctx context.Context, entry *MountEntry, updateStorage bool) error {
65: 	// Ensure we end the path in a slash
66: 	if !strings.HasSuffix(entry.Path, "/") {
67: 		entry.Path += "/"
68: 	}
69: 
70: 	// Ensure there is a name
71: 	if entry.Path == "/" {
72: 		return fmt.Errorf("backend path must be specified")
73: 	}
74: 
75: 	// Update the audit table
76: 	c.auditLock.Lock()
77: 	defer c.auditLock.Unlock()
78: 
79: 	// Look for matching name
80: 	for _, ent := range c.audit.Entries {
81: 		switch {
82: 		// Existing is sql/mysql/ new is sql/ or
83: 		// existing is sql/ and new is sql/mysql/
84: 		case strings.HasPrefix(ent.Path, entry.Path):
85: 			fallthrough
86: 		case strings.HasPrefix(entry.Path, ent.Path):
87: 			return fmt.Errorf("path already in use")
88: 		}
89: 	}
90: 
91: 	// Generate a new UUID and view
92: 	if entry.UUID == "" {
93: 		entryUUID, err := uuid.GenerateUUID()
94: 		if err != nil {
95: 			return err
96: 		}
97: 		entry.UUID = entryUUID
98: 	}
99: 	if entry.Accessor == "" {
100: 		accessor, err := c.generateMountAccessor("audit_" + entry.Type)
101: 		if err != nil {
102: 			return err
103: 		}
104: 		entry.Accessor = accessor
105: 	}
106: 	viewPath := entry.ViewPath()
107: 	view := NewBarrierView(c.barrier, viewPath)
108: 	addAuditPathChecker(c, entry, view, viewPath)
109: 	origViewReadOnlyErr := view.getReadOnlyErr()
110: 
111: 	// Mark the view as read-only until the mounting is complete and
112: 	// ensure that it is reset after. This ensures that there will be no
113: 	// writes during the construction of the backend.
114: 	view.setReadOnlyErr(logical.ErrSetupReadOnly)
115: 	defer view.setReadOnlyErr(origViewReadOnlyErr)
116: 
117: 	// Lookup the new backend
118: 	backend, err := c.newAuditBackend(ctx, entry, view, entry.Options)
119: 	if err != nil {
120: 		return err
121: 	}
122: 	if backend == nil {
123: 		return fmt.Errorf("nil audit backend of type %q returned from factory", entry.Type)
124: 	}
125: 
126: 	if entry.Options["skip_test"] != "true" {
127: 		// Test the new audit device and report failure if it doesn't work.
128: 		testProbe, err := c.generateAuditTestProbe()
129: 		if err != nil {
130: 			return err
131: 		}
132: 		err = backend.LogTestMessage(ctx, testProbe, entry.Options)
133: 		if err != nil {
134: 			c.logger.Error("new audit backend failed test", "path", entry.Path, "type", entry.Type, "error", err)
135: 			return fmt.Errorf("audit backend failed test message: %w", err)
136: 
137: 		}
138: 	}
139: 
140: 	newTable := c.audit.shallowClone()
141: 	newTable.Entries = append(newTable.Entries, entry)
142: 
143: 	ns, err := namespace.FromContext(ctx)
144: 	if err != nil {
145: 		return err
146: 	}
147: 	entry.NamespaceID = ns.ID
148: 	entry.namespace = ns
149: 
150: 	if updateStorage {
151: 		if err := c.persistAudit(ctx, newTable, entry.Local); err != nil {
152: 			return errors.New("failed to update audit table")
153: 		}
154: 	}
155: 
156: 	c.audit = newTable
157: 
158: 	// Register the backend
159: 	c.auditBroker.Register(entry.Path, backend, entry.Local, c.IsExperimentEnabled(experiments.VaultExperimentCoreAuditEventsAlpha1))
160: 	if c.logger.IsInfo() {
161: 		c.logger.Info("enabled audit backend", "path", entry.Path, "type", entry.Type)
162: 	}
163: 
164: 	return nil
165: }

173: 
174: 	// Ensure there is a name
175: 	if path == "/" {
176: 		return false, fmt.Errorf("backend path must be specified")
177: 	}
178: 
179: 	// Remove the entry from the mount table
180: 	c.auditLock.Lock()
181: 	defer c.auditLock.Unlock()
182: 
183: 	newTable := c.audit.shallowClone()
184: 	entry, err := newTable.remove(ctx, path)
185: 	if err != nil {
186: 		return false, err
187: 	}
188: 
189: 	// Ensure there was a match
190: 	if entry == nil {
191: 		return false, fmt.Errorf("no matching backend")
192: 	}
193: 
194: 	c.removeAuditReloadFunc(entry)
195: 
196: 	// When unmounting all entries the JSON code will load back up from storage
197: 	// as a nil slice, which kills tests...just set it nil explicitly
198: 	if len(newTable.Entries) == 0 {
199: 		newTable.Entries = nil
200: 	}
201: 
202: 	if updateStorage {
203: 		// Update the audit table
204: 		if err := c.persistAudit(ctx, newTable, entry.Local); err != nil {
205: 			return true, errors.New("failed to update audit table")
206: 		}
207: 	}
208: 
209: 	c.audit = newTable
210: 
211: 	// Unmount the backend
212: 	c.auditBroker.Deregister(path, c.IsExperimentEnabled(experiments.VaultExperimentCoreAuditEventsAlpha1))
213: 	if c.logger.IsInfo() {
214: 		c.logger.Info("disabled audit backend", "path", path)
215: 	}
216: 
217: 	removeAuditPathChecker(c, entry)
218: 
219: 	return true, nil
220: }
221: 
222: // loadAudits is invoked as part of postUnseal to load the audit table
223: func (c *Core) loadAudits(ctx context.Context) error {
224: 	auditTable := &MountTable{}
225: 	localAuditTable := &MountTable{}
226: 
227: 	// Load the existing audit table
228: 	raw, err := c.barrier.Get(ctx, coreAuditConfigPath)
229: 	if err != nil {
230: 		c.logger.Error("failed to read audit table", "error", err)
231: 		return errLoadAuditFailed
232: 	}
233: 	rawLocal, err := c.barrier.Get(ctx, coreLocalAuditConfigPath)
234: 	if err != nil {
235: 		c.logger.Error("failed to read local audit table", "error", err)
236: 		return errLoadAuditFailed
237: 	}
238: 
239: 	c.auditLock.Lock()
240: 	defer c.auditLock.Unlock()
241: 
242: 	if raw != nil {
243: 		if err := jsonutil.DecodeJSON(raw.Value, auditTable); err != nil {
244: 			c.logger.Error("failed to decode audit table", "error", err)
245: 			return errLoadAuditFailed
246: 		}
247: 		c.audit = auditTable
248: 	}
249: 
250: 	var needPersist bool
251: 	if c.audit == nil {
252: 		c.audit = defaultAuditTable()
253: 		needPersist = true
254: 	}
255: 
256: 	if rawLocal != nil {
257: 		if err := jsonutil.DecodeJSON(rawLocal.Value, localAuditTable); err != nil {
258: 			c.logger.Error("failed to decode local audit table", "error", err)
259: 			return errLoadAuditFailed
260: 		}
261: 		if localAuditTable != nil && len(localAuditTable.Entries) > 0 {
262: 			c.audit.Entries = append(c.audit.Entries, localAuditTable.Entries...)
263: 		}
264: 	}
265: 
266: 	// Upgrade to typed auth table
267: 	if c.audit.Type == "" {
268: 		c.audit.Type = auditTableType
269: 		needPersist = true
270: 	}
271: 
272: 	// Upgrade to table-scoped entries
273: 	for _, entry := range c.audit.Entries {
274: 		if entry.Table == "" {
275: 			entry.Table = c.audit.Type
276: 			needPersist = true
277: 		}
278: 		if entry.Accessor == "" {
279: 			accessor, err := c.generateMountAccessor("audit_" + entry.Type)
280: 			if err != nil {
281: 				return err
282: 			}
283: 			entry.Accessor = accessor
284: 			needPersist = true
285: 		}
286: 
287: 		if entry.NamespaceID == "" {
288: 			entry.NamespaceID = namespace.RootNamespaceID
289: 			needPersist = true
290: 		}
291: 		// Get the namespace from the namespace ID and load it in memory
292: 		ns, err := NamespaceByID(ctx, entry.NamespaceID, c)
293: 		if err != nil {
294: 			return err
295: 		}
296: 		if ns == nil {
297: 			return namespace.ErrNoNamespace
298: 		}
299: 		entry.namespace = ns
300: 	}
301: 
302: 	if !needPersist || c.perfStandby {
303: 		return nil
304: 	}
305: 
306: 	if err := c.persistAudit(ctx, c.audit, false); err != nil {
307: 		return errLoadAuditFailed
308: 	}
309: 	return nil
310: }
311: 
312: // persistAudit is used to persist the audit table after modification
313: func (c *Core) persistAudit(ctx context.Context, table *MountTable, localOnly bool) error {
314: 	if table.Type != auditTableType {
315: 		c.logger.Error("given table to persist has wrong type", "actual_type", table.Type, "expected_type", auditTableType)
316: 		return fmt.Errorf("invalid table type given, not persisting")
317: 	}
318: 
319: 	nonLocalAudit := &MountTable{
320: 		Type: auditTableType,
321: 	}
322: 
323: 	localAudit := &MountTable{
324: 		Type: auditTableType,
325: 	}
326: 
327: 	for _, entry := range table.Entries {
328: 		if entry.Table != table.Type {
329: 			c.logger.Error("given entry to persist in audit table has wrong table value", "path", entry.Path, "entry_table_type", entry.Table, "actual_type", table.Type)
330: 			return fmt.Errorf("invalid audit entry found, not persisting")
331: 		}
332: 
333: 		if entry.Local {
334: 			localAudit.Entries = append(localAudit.Entries, entry)
335: 		} else {
336: 			nonLocalAudit.Entries = append(nonLocalAudit.Entries, entry)
337: 		}
338: 	}
339: 
340: 	if !localOnly {
341: 		// Marshal the table
342: 		compressedBytes, err := jsonutil.EncodeJSONAndCompress(nonLocalAudit, nil)
343: 		if err != nil {
344: 			c.logger.Error("failed to encode and/or compress audit table", "error", err)
345: 			return err
346: 		}
347: 
348: 		// Create an entry
349: 		entry := &logical.StorageEntry{
350: 			Key:   coreAuditConfigPath,
351: 			Value: compressedBytes,
352: 		}
353: 
354: 		// Write to the physical backend
355: 		if err := c.barrier.Put(ctx, entry); err != nil {
356: 			c.logger.Error("failed to persist audit table", "error", err)
357: 			return err
358: 		}
359: 	}
360: 
361: 	// Repeat with local audit
362: 	compressedBytes, err := jsonutil.EncodeJSONAndCompress(localAudit, nil)
363: 	if err != nil {
364: 		c.logger.Error("failed to encode and/or compress local audit table", "error", err)
365: 		return err
366: 	}
367: 
368: 	entry := &logical.StorageEntry{
369: 		Key:   coreLocalAuditConfigPath,
370: 		Value: compressedBytes,
371: 	}
372: 
373: 	if err := c.barrier.Put(ctx, entry); err != nil {
374: 		c.logger.Error("failed to persist local audit table", "error", err)
375: 		return err
376: 	}
377: 
378: 	return nil
379: }
380: 
381: // setupAudit is invoked after we've loaded the audit able to
382: // initialize the audit backends
383: func (c *Core) setupAudits(ctx context.Context) error {
384: 	brokerLogger := c.baseLogger.Named("audit")
385: 	c.AddLogger(brokerLogger)
386: 	broker := NewAuditBroker(brokerLogger)
387: 
388: 	c.auditLock.Lock()
389: 	defer c.auditLock.Unlock()
390: 
391: 	var successCount int
392: 
393: 	for _, entry := range c.audit.Entries {
394: 		// Create a barrier view using the UUID
395: 		viewPath := entry.ViewPath()
396: 		view := NewBarrierView(c.barrier, viewPath)
397: 		addAuditPathChecker(c, entry, view, viewPath)
398: 		origViewReadOnlyErr := view.getReadOnlyErr()
399: 
400: 		// Mark the view as read-only until the mounting is complete and
401: 		// ensure that it is reset after. This ensures that there will be no
402: 		// writes during the construction of the backend.
403: 		view.setReadOnlyErr(logical.ErrSetupReadOnly)
404: 		c.postUnsealFuncs = append(c.postUnsealFuncs, func() {
405: 			view.setReadOnlyErr(origViewReadOnlyErr)
406: 		})
407: 
408: 		// Initialize the backend
409: 		backend, err := c.newAuditBackend(ctx, entry, view, entry.Options)
410: 		if err != nil {
411: 			c.logger.Error("failed to create audit entry", "path", entry.Path, "error", err)
412: 			continue
413: 		}
414: 		if backend == nil {
415: 			c.logger.Error("created audit entry was nil", "path", entry.Path, "type", entry.Type)
416: 			continue
417: 		}
418: 
419: 		// Mount the backend
420: 		broker.Register(entry.Path, backend, entry.Local, c.IsExperimentEnabled(experiments.VaultExperimentCoreAuditEventsAlpha1))
421: 
422: 		successCount++
423: 	}
424: 
425: 	if len(c.audit.Entries) > 0 && successCount == 0 {
426: 		return errLoadAuditFailed
427: 	}
428: 
429: 	c.auditBroker = broker
430: 	return nil
431: }
432: 
433: // teardownAudit is used before we seal the vault to reset the audit
434: // backends to their unloaded state. This is reversed by loadAudits.
435: func (c *Core) teardownAudits() error {
436: 	c.auditLock.Lock()
437: 	defer c.auditLock.Unlock()
438: 
439: 	if c.audit != nil {
440: 		for _, entry := range c.audit.Entries {
441: 			c.removeAuditReloadFunc(entry)
442: 			removeAuditPathChecker(c, entry)
443: 		}
444: 	}
445: 
446: 	c.audit = nil
447: 	c.auditBroker = nil
448: 	return nil
449: }
450: 
451: // removeAuditReloadFunc removes the reload func from the working set. The
452: // audit lock needs to be held before calling this.
453: func (c *Core) removeAuditReloadFunc(entry *MountEntry) {
454: 	switch entry.Type {
455: 	case "file":
456: 		key := "audit_file|" + entry.Path
457: 		c.reloadFuncsLock.Lock()
458: 
459: 		if c.logger.IsDebug() {
460: 			c.baseLogger.Named("audit").Debug("removing reload function", "path", entry.Path)
461: 		}
462: 
463: 		delete(c.reloadFuncs, key)
464: 
465: 		c.reloadFuncsLock.Unlock()
466: 	}
467: }
468: 
469: // newAuditBackend is used to create and configure a new audit backend by name
470: func (c *Core) newAuditBackend(ctx context.Context, entry *MountEntry, view logical.Storage, conf map[string]string) (audit.Backend, error) {
471: 	f, ok := c.auditBackends[entry.Type]
472: 	if !ok {
473: 		return nil, fmt.Errorf("unknown backend type: %q", entry.Type)
474: 	}
475: 	saltConfig := &salt.Config{
476: 		HMAC:     sha256.New,
477: 		HMACType: "hmac-sha256",
478: 		Location: salt.DefaultLocation,
479: 	}
480: 
481: 	be, err := f(ctx, &audit.BackendConfig{
482: 		SaltView:   view,
483: 		SaltConfig: saltConfig,
484: 		Config:     conf,
485: 	})
486: 	if err != nil {
487: 		return nil, err
488: 	}
489: 	if be == nil {
490: 		return nil, fmt.Errorf("nil backend returned from %q factory function", entry.Type)
491: 	}
492: 
493: 	auditLogger := c.baseLogger.Named("audit")
494: 	c.AddLogger(auditLogger)
495: 
496: 	switch entry.Type {
497: 	case "file":
498: 		key := "audit_file|" + entry.Path
499: 
500: 		c.reloadFuncsLock.Lock()
501: 
502: 		if auditLogger.IsDebug() {
503: 			auditLogger.Debug("adding reload function", "path", entry.Path)
504: 			if entry.Options != nil {
505: 				auditLogger.Debug("file backend options", "path", entry.Path, "file_path", entry.Options["file_path"])
506: 			}
507: 		}
508: 
509: 		c.reloadFuncs[key] = append(c.reloadFuncs[key], func() error {
510: 			if auditLogger.IsInfo() {
511: 				auditLogger.Info("reloading file audit backend", "path", entry.Path)
512: 			}
513: 			return be.Reload(ctx)
514: 		})
515: 
516: 		c.reloadFuncsLock.Unlock()
517: 	case "socket":
518: 		if auditLogger.IsDebug() {
519: 			if entry.Options != nil {
520: 				auditLogger.Debug("socket backend options", "path", entry.Path, "address", entry.Options["address"], "socket type", entry.Options["socket_type"])
521: 			}
522: 		}
523: 	case "syslog":
524: 		if auditLogger.IsDebug() {
525: 			if entry.Options != nil {
526: 				auditLogger.Debug("syslog backend options", "path", entry.Path, "facility", entry.Options["facility"], "tag", entry.Options["tag"])
527: 			}
528: 		}
529: 	}
530: 
531: 	return be, err
532: }
533: 
534: // defaultAuditTable creates a default audit table
535: func defaultAuditTable() *MountTable {
536: 	table := &MountTable{
537: 		Type: auditTableType,
538: 	}
539: 	return table
540: }

32: // RetryUntil runs f until it returns a nil result or the timeout is reached.
33: // If a nil result hasn't been obtained by timeout, calls t.Fatal.
34: func RetryUntil(t testing.T, timeout time.Duration, f func() error) {
35: 	t.Helper()
36: 	deadline := time.Now().Add(timeout)
37: 	var err error
38: 	for time.Now().Before(deadline) {
39: 		if err = f(); err == nil {
40: 			return
41: 		}
42: 		time.Sleep(100 * time.Millisecond)
43: 	}
44: 	t.Fatalf("did not complete before deadline, err: %v", err)
45: }

301: func (n *NoopAudit) LogRequest(ctx context.Context, in *logical.LogInput) error {

304: 	if n.formatter != nil {

307: 		if err != nil {

309: 		}
310: 		n.records = append(n.records, w.Bytes())
311: 	}

314: 	n.Req = append(n.Req, in.Request)
315: 	n.ReqHeaders = append(n.ReqHeaders, in.Request.Headers)
316: 	n.ReqNonHMACKeys = in.NonHMACReqDataKeys
317: 	n.ReqErrs = append(n.ReqErrs, in.OuterErr)

426: 
427: 	// We send nothing on the regular logger, that way we can later deregister
428: 	// the sink to stop logging during cluster cleanup.
429: 	logger := hclog.NewInterceptLogger(&hclog.LoggerOptions{
430: 		Output:            ioutil.Discard,

432: 	})

434: 		Output:            output,

437: 	})

442: 		Logger: logger,
443: 		sink:   sink,
444: 	}
